market_regime.label: GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED_OVERSOLD_WEEKEND_BINARY_PENDING
market_regime.key_risk: Weekend Israel-Iran escalation binary + 3-day Nifty decline (-2.78%) primes Monday for binary-driven open. Brent closed $100.71 (-4.15% intraday Fri) — cooled but in elevated $100-108 zone. IT sector cascade (INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% Fri) needs stabilization. FII selling 4th day ~₹3,254 cr.
market_regime.dominant_risk: Weekend binary (Israel strike threats, Hormuz still blocked). Any fresh escalation headline = Monday gap-down -1 to -2%. Any ceasefire re-affirmation = relief bounce. Below psychological 24,000 / 23,900 forecast floor breached — 23,800 now key support.
market_regime.crude_note: Brent cooled -4.15% Friday to $100.71 from $105.07. Still elevated ($100-108 zone). Needs <$95 for regime relief, >$108 signals weekend escalation. Intraday cooling suggests some ceasefire-repair noise.
market_regime.regime_shift_note: Apr 23 regime label had been GEOPOLITICAL_RISK_ELEVATED_CEASEFIRE_FRAYING_HORMUZ_ACTIVE. Apr 24 shifted to OVERSOLD_WEEKEND_BINARY_PENDING after Brent cooled intraday but IT sector cascade dominated. Monday label will depend on weekend headlines.

session_direction.key_assumption: Brent surge to $105-106 overnight negates EOD 'oversold bounce' thesis. Regime back to RISK_ELEVATED with Israel-Iran escalation concerns. Weekend binary event risk (Israel attacks threatened, Hormuz shipping blocked) drives Friday risk-off positioning — typical 'sell into weekend' flow. IT sector continues under pressure (Nifty IT -4.95% post-HCLTech guidance shock), no fresh catalyst to reverse. FII selling extended to 4th consecutive day (~₹3,254 cr). Key support 24,000 (psychological + recent swing); if breaks, 23,900 next. Upside capped at 24,200-24,220. Defense (ASTRA MICRO, MAZDOCK) likely to firm on geopolitical premium. OMC/Aviation (MRPL, INDIGO) under pressure from Brent surge. Mean-reversion bounce possible intraday but unlikely to hold into close.

FII selling accelerates on 4th consecutive day — ₹5,000+ cr outflow scenario

scenarios.description: Morning weakness bottoms 24,050-24,080, mid-session short-cover bounce to 24,180-24,200, fades in last hour on weekend positioning. Closes flat to -0.3%.

scenarios.description: Fresh Israel-Iran headline intraday, Brent to $110+, Nifty breaks 24,000 decisively, closes 23,850-23,950. Defense ripper, IT decimated.

ceasefire_binary_matrix.note: Intraday Brent cool (-4.15%) suggests some ceasefire-repair noise. Collapse probability eased from 55% to 40%. Weekend window is the binary decider — Mon morning check: Israel headlines, Hormuz status, overnight US statements.

stocks_to_watch: INFY: After -6.93% Fri (₹1,154.60) — any weekend tech guidance chatter + Monday open will set IT sector tone. Watch for reclaim of ₹1,200 or breakdown to ₹1,100.
HCLTECH: ₹1,203.20 (-4.02% Fri). P028 target <₹1,430 already achieved (check Apr 28). Below ₹1,200 = M013 confirmation 6th time.
NIFTY IT Index: Fri close ~28,500 range. Lost -4.95% over 2 sessions. Weekend TCS/Wipro headlines matter.
ASTRA MICRO: ₹1,123.75 (-1.04% Fri). P030 target 1,010-1,060 still well above. Watch for break of ₹1,100 if ceasefire re-affirms; firm if escalation.
COCHINSHIP: ₹1,664.70 (+4.51% Fri) — Friday's defense standout. Monday follow-through possible on any weekend headlines.
MAZDOCK: ₹2,680.40 (-0.50% Fri). Defense basket laggard — watch for rotation back in on escalation; trim on flat weekend.
SETFGOLD: ₹127.73. P029 graded PARTIAL. Haven bid durability check — Monday open reveals residual premium.
MRPL / INDIGO: OMC + aviation pressure if Brent back above $103 Monday. Relief if Brent <$98. MRPL ₹186.38, INDIGO ₹4,523.10.
BankNifty: 56,089.75 — critical test of 56,000 support. Break = credit rotation worsens. Reclaim 56,500 = relief.
IRFC: ₹103.92. P010 target 85-95 check Apr 29 — tracking WRONG.
prompt_for_morning_brief: MORNING BRIEF 2026-04-27 (Monday): (1) Score opening bias — EOD forecast FLAT_TO_MILD_GAP_DOWN (-0.25%, GIFT est 23840, confidence 2/5). Compare against ACTUAL NSE open. (2) CRITICAL: Scan weekend for Israel-Iran headlines (strikes? ceasefire re-affirmation?), Hormuz shipping status, Brent overnight level. Binary outcome reshapes the day. (3) Friday Apr 24 session result: Nifty -1.14% to 23,897.95 (3rd bearish day, cumulative -2.78% over Apr 22-24). Morning-brief forecast BEARISH_VOLATILE (-0.6%) graded PARTIAL_CORRECT — direction right, close 2 pts below range low, magnitude ~1.9x base; Brent forecast $103-108 was WRONG (actual $100.71, -4.15%). (4) Predictions closed Apr 24: P001 WRONG, P004 WRONG, P027 WRONG, P029 PARTIAL. New accuracy: 15.0% (3 right / 20 scored). (5) DEFENSE_DURATION_PREMIUM (M012) now ready for framework promotion — 3 occurrences (P007, P009, P004). (6) Regime: OVERSOLD_WEEKEND_BINARY_PENDING. IT sector support check key; 23,800 is pivotal Nifty support; BankNifty 56,000 must hold.

opening_bias_actual.gift_nifty_note: GIFT Nifty showed 24,124.5 (-0.79% on its own reference) pre-open on April 24 02:30 IST, signalling GAP DOWN vs prev Nifty close 24,173.05. Materially below EOD forecast estimate (24,210).
opening_bias_actual.scoring_note: Forecast FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP (+0.15%, GIFT est 24,210). Actual gap -0.24% (24,115.55 vs prev close 24,173.05) — GAP_DOWN direction opposite of forecast. Overnight regime change: Brent surged +7-8% from $97.76 close to $105-106 (Israel threatens Iran attacks, Hormuz shipping remains blocked, US-Iran ceasefire fraying). The constructive 'oversold bounce' thesis collapsed on Brent surge. FII selling extended to 4th session (~₹3,254 cr). Graded WRONG — direction opposite, magnitude off by 0.4pts. Missed signal: overnight Hormuz escalation risk materialized despite modeled 35% probability.

opening_bias.rationale_note: Confidence 2/5 because weekend binary risk (Israel-Iran escalation) can swing open ±1% in either direction. Base case leans mildly negative on continued FII outflow + below-psychological close. Any ceasefire re-affirmation overnight could flip to gap-up.

session_direction.key_assumption: Oversold after 3 consecutive bearish sessions (Apr 22-24 cumulative -2.78%). Monday typically sees either continuation-capitulation (breakdown of 23,800-23,900) or mean-reversion bounce (reclaim 24,000). Base case assumes weekend passes without major Israel-Iran strike, Brent holds $98-105, and IT sector stabilizes near lows without fresh negative guidance. Mean-reversion bounce likely intraday but upside capped at 24,100 without a fresh positive catalyst. FII selling 5th day possible; DII support at 23,800 expected. Key binary: weekend geopolitical headlines.
session_direction.brent_forecast: $97-105 — base case Brent drifts in this band on status-quo weekend. Break <$95 = constructive (gap-up Monday); break >$108 = escalation (gap-down ≥0.7%).

scenarios.name: ESCALATION_GAP_DOWN (20%)
scenarios.description: Weekend Israel-Iran escalation, Brent >$108. Monday gaps down -1 to -2%, tests 23,500-23,600. Defense ripper, IT/OMC smashed.

session_direction_accuracy_today.direction_note: Forecast BEARISH_VOLATILE (morning-brief revision, -0.6%, range 23,900-24,220). Actual: Nifty closed 23,897.95 (-1.14% from 24,173.05). Direction CORRECT (bearish confirmed). Close 23,897.95 is 2 pts BELOW range low (23,900) — essentially AT the forecast range edge. Magnitude ~1.9x stronger than -0.6% base case; actual fell into the ESCALATION_SPIRAL scenario range (23,850-23,950, 15% prob). NOTE: Escalation scenario trigger (Brent >$110) did NOT fire — Brent actually cooled -4.15% intraday to $100.71. Driver was IT sector cascade (INFY -6.93%, HCLTECH -4.02% follow-through) + weekend risk-off positioning, not geopolitical re-escalation.
session_direction_accuracy_today.range_note: Close 23,897.95 vs forecast range 23,900-24,220. 2.05 pts below range low — ESSENTIALLY AT edge. Counts as marginal range breach (within 0.01%).
session_direction_accuracy_today.magnitude_note: Forecast -0.6% base, scenarios spanned -0.3% to -1.3%. Actual -1.14% falls in escalation-spiral scenario but exceeded base case by ~0.54pts.
session_direction_accuracy_today.brent_note: Forecast Brent $103-108 sustained. Actual $100.71 (-4.15% intraday). Brent forecast WRONG on direction — cooled rather than stayed elevated. Yet Nifty still sold off — key insight: session was dominated by IT sector cascade, not crude macro. FORECAST_PAT007 candidate: idiosyncratic sector drag overrides macro thesis.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scoring_note: Direction CORRECT (bearish). Close marginally below range low (2 pts, 0.01%). Magnitude ~1.9x base case but within scenario envelope. Brent forecast WRONG on direction. Overall PARTIAL_CORRECT — the session-direction call held but macro driver attribution was off.
session_direction_accuracy_today.scenarios_hit: ESCALATION_SPIRAL range (23,850-23,950) — close 23,897.95 sits squarely inside. Scenario was assigned 15% prob; reality triggered via IT cascade rather than Brent spike (different mechanism, same price path).
